            operations++;
        }
        operations += (startValue - target);
        return operations;
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int brokenCalc(int startValue, int target) {
        int operations = 0;
        while (target > startValue) {
            if (target % 2 == 1) {
                target += 1;
                operations++;
            }
            target /= 2;
            operations++;
        }
        operations += (startValue - target);
        return operations;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log(target))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The approach works backward from the target to the startValue, preferring to divide by 2 (reverse of multiplying) or add 1 (reverse of subtracting) when the target is odd. This minimizes operations by reducing the target efficiently. Strengths include logarithmic time complexity and optimal step selection. Weaknesses are limited to integer division and addition, which may not handle all edge cases without careful adjustment.
